title: Extend the TikTok Deadline Act
plain_summary: This bill gives TikTok more time to find a new owner before it gets banned in the United States. Right now, TikTok has 270 days to be sold to someone else. This bill would give them 540 days instead, which is almost twice as long. That means TikTok would have about nine more months to find a buyer and avoid being shut down in America.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Extend the TikTok Deadline Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Extension of Deadline
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act. The current law gives TikTok two hundred seventy days to be sold to avoid being banned. This bill changes that time limit to five hundred forty days instead. This means TikTok would get an extra two hundred seventy days to find a buyer before the ban takes effect.
